sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Disney shares are trading at a discount. Raymond James says it's time to buy
feed.summary: Raymond James has upgraded Disney to outperform from market perform, setting a $115 price target, believing the stock is trading at an attractive valuation despite macro fears and expected theme park attendance headwinds. The firm anticipates benefits from new cruise ships, a Disneyland Paris expansion, and growth in Disney's streaming business, which could offset park-related challenges. Most analysts on Wall Street also have a positive outlook on Disney stock.

feed.title: Omnicom CEO John Wren earned $70m package in 2025
feed.summary: Omnicom CEO John Wren received a nearly $70 million pay package in 2025, primarily through stock options, as part of a new incentive structure designed to align his compensation directly with Omnicom's stock performance. Wren reduced his salary to $1, receiving options allowing him to profit only if the company's share price rises above $77.60. This move followed the $9 billion acquisition of Interpublic and aims to ensure Wren's continued leadership during a period of significant transformation for the agency group.

feed.title: RTX unit's new tool tests hidden networks before lives are at risk
feed.summary: RTX BBN Technologies, a unit of RTX (NYSE: RTX), has released Maude-HCS, an open-source toolkit for modeling, testing, and validating covert communication networks. Funded by DARPA, this tool significantly reduces validation time from weeks to hours with a low error rate of 1%-9%. Maude-HCS aims to strengthen U.S. cyber defense by ensuring the effectiveness and undetectability of critical hidden communication systems used in national security and contested environments.

feed.title: Hitting a buried power or gas line? Dominion says call 811 first
feed.summary: Dominion Energy is urging its Virginia and North Carolina customers to call 811, the "Call Before You Dig" hotline, before starting any outdoor digging projects this spring. This reminder coincides with National Safe Digging Month in April and aims to prevent accidental strikes on underground utility lines, which can lead to service disruptions, fines, property damage, or serious injury. Virginia and North Carolina law requires contacting 811 at least three business days before any excavation work.

feed.title: United Airlines posts Q1 results Apr. 21, webcast follows Apr. 22
feed.summary: United Airlines (NYSE: UAL) will release its first-quarter 2026 financial results after market close on Tuesday, April 21, 2026. The company will then host a live, listen-only webcast conference call to discuss these results and outlook on Wednesday, April 22, 2026, at 9:30 a.m. CT / 10:30 a.m. ET. The webcast will be accessible through the investor relations section of United's website, ir.united.com.

feed.title: Citigroup Adjusts Price Target on Textron to $99 From $97, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: Citigroup has adjusted its price target for Textron (TXT) shares to $99, an increase from the previous target of $97. The firm has decided to maintain a "Neutral" rating on the stock. This update reflects Citigroup's latest assessment of Textron's valuation and outlook.

feed.title: Omnicom launches Adobe practice, integrates LeapPoint By Investing.com
feed.summary: Omnicom Group Inc. announced the launch of a new Adobe practice through its transformation consultancy, Credera, integrating its 2024 acquisition, LeapPoint. This move expands Omnicom's digital transformation capabilities and aims to help clients maximize their Adobe investments. The new practice will be presented at Adobe's Summit in April.

feed.title: Kurt Sievers, Retired CEO of NXP Semiconductors, Appointed to Booking Holdings Board
feed.summary: Booking Holdings Inc. announced the appointment of Kurt Sievers, former CEO of NXP Semiconductors, to its Board of Directors. Sievers brings extensive global experience in technology, mobility, and consumer markets, having led major initiatives at NXP including key acquisitions and mergers. Concurrently, Lynn Radakovich will retire from the Booking Holdings Board at the upcoming Annual Meeting in June.

feed.title: Broadridge Financial Solutions stock hits 52-week low at 156.77 USD
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions (BR) stock has fallen to a new 52-week low of 156.66 USD, marking a 32.45% decline over the past year. Despite this, InvestingPro data suggests the stock may be undervalued and highlights that the company has raised its dividend for 19 consecutive years. Recent news includes an acquisition to enhance trading technology and analyst ratings adjustments, with several firms maintaining Buy or Outperform ratings despite lower price targets.

feed.title: Form FWP CITIGROUP INC Filed by: CITIGROUP INC
feed.summary: Citigroup Global Markets Holdings Inc., guaranteed by Citigroup Inc., has filed a Form FWP for 5 Year Autocallable Market-Linked Securities tied to the Citi Dynamic Asset Selector 5 Excess Return Index (CIISDA5N). These securities offer a 100.00% upside participation rate and an automatic early redemption feature with a premium of at least 6.25% per annum, subject to the index meeting certain levels. The filing details the product's terms, hypothetical scenarios, underlying index methodology, and specific risk considerations, including credit risk and the volatility of the index.

feed.title: New CEO Shelves Breakup — Is Kraft Heinz Finally a Turnaround Stock Worth Owning?
feed.summary: Kraft Heinz's new CEO, Steve Cahillane, has abandoned plans to break up the company, opting instead to invest $600 million into the core business. This decision aligns with Warren Buffett's earlier opposition to a split and positions Kraft Heinz to stabilize volumes amid challenges like GLP-1 drugs and private label competition. Despite a GAAP net loss, the company generated strong free cash flow and offers an attractive dividend yield, making it a potential turnaround stock.

feed.title: CoStar Group stock hits 52-week low at $39.10
feed.summary: CoStar Group (CSGP) stock has fallen to a 52-week low of $39.10, representing a 50% decrease over the past year. Despite the significant downturn, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is undervalued, with the company holding more cash than debt and expecting net income growth. Recent developments include an expansion of Homes.com, a significant share purchase by the CEO, and board appointment, alongside criticism regarding reduced financial transparency.

feed.title: Hasbro investigates cybersecurity incident, takes some systems offline
feed.summary: Hasbro is currently investigating a cybersecurity incident that led to some of its systems being taken offline. The company has engaged with law enforcement and third-party cybersecurity experts to address the breach. While specific details about the nature and impact of the incident are not yet fully disclosed, Hasbro is working to restore affected systems and ensure the security of its operations.

feed.title: Aflac Signs First External Reinsurance Deal With Japan Post Insurance
feed.summary: Aflac's subsidiary, Aflac Re Bermuda Ltd., has finalized its first external reinsurance deal with Japan Post Insurance, covering a block of whole-life annuity policies. This strategic move, effective March 31, 2026, aims to enhance Aflac's capital efficiency, diversify revenue streams, and expand its presence in third-party reinsurance. Japan Post Insurance will continue to manage the policies, while Aflac Re assumes a portion of the risk.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Lowers Invesco (NYSE:IVZ) Price Target to $26.00
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has reduced its price target for Invesco (NYSE:IVZ) from $28.00 to $26.00, while maintaining an "equal weight" rating, suggesting a 5.49% potential upside from the current share price. This adjustment comes despite Invesco recently beating quarterly earnings estimates with $0.62 EPS on revenue of $1.26 billion. The consensus among analysts remains a "Hold" with an average target of $28.25, although some firms like Deutsche Bank, Argus, and JPMorgan have issued higher price targets for Invesco in recent months.

feed.title: Citigroup Inc Stock (C) Opened Up by 7.41% on Apr 1: Drivers Behind the Movement
feed.summary: Citigroup Inc. (C) shares surged 7.41% on April 1, driven by positive analyst upgrades, significant institutional buying, and progress in resolving regulatory issues. The company's finalized exit from Russia and strong Q4 earnings, along with optimistic future guidance, further boosted investor confidence. Despite a "neutral" MACD and RSI, the stock's fundamentals, including $81.18B in annual revenue, indicate strong performance within the Banking & Investment Services sector.

feed.title: Japan Post Holdings sells Aflac shares worth $1.59 million By Investing.com
feed.summary: Japan Post Holdings Co., Ltd., a ten percent owner of AFLAC INC (NYSE:AFL), sold 14,700 shares of common stock on March 30, 2026, for approximately $1.59 million. This transaction was reported in a Form 4 filing with the SEC. Following the sale, Japan Post Holdings still indirectly owns over 51 million shares of Aflac, while analysts like Mizuho have recently adjusted price targets and maintained an "Underperform" rating on Aflac's stock.

feed.title: CarMax Recognized as One of the 100 Best Companies to Work For® for the 22nd Consecutive Year
feed.summary: CarMax has been honored by Fortune and Great Place to Work® as one of the 100 Best Companies to Work For® for the 22nd consecutive year, ranking #56 in 2026. This recognition highlights CarMax's strong workplace culture, built on trust and support among its associates, and its commitment to employee satisfaction. The company also received accolades as one of the "Best Workplaces in Retail" and for "Parents," and is actively hiring for various roles.

feed.title: PTC Inc. Announces That BMW Group Has Successfully Implemented Ptc's Codebeamer Application Lifecycle Management Solution as the Foundation for Its Next Generation of Digital Engineering
feed.summary: PTC Inc. announced that BMW Group has successfully implemented PTC's Codebeamer application lifecycle management (ALM) solution as the core of its next-generation digital engineering. Codebeamer will serve as an enterprise-wide solution for requirements management, providing consistent processes, stronger traceability, and digital continuity across various engineering disciplines. This implementation will facilitate integrated mechatronics development, improve supplier collaboration, and support BMW Group's adoption of AI-enabled engineering workflows.

feed.title: Hershey u‑turns as real chocolate makes a comeback
feed.summary: The Hershey Company has announced it is reintroducing "real chocolate" into a small percentage of its Reese's and Hershey's products, affecting less than three percent of Reese's items. This shift comes amid mounting backlash over ingredient changes, though CEO Kirk Tanner states the decision was made before recent public criticism. The move signals a broader industry trend towards quality ingredients and transparency.

feed.title: MEDIA ALERT: Equinix Sets Conference Call for First-Quarter Results
feed.summary: Equinix, Inc. announced it will host a conference call on Wednesday, April 29, 2026, at 5:30 p.m. ET to discuss its first-quarter results for the period ending March 31, 2026. Interested parties can access the call live via phone or webcast on Equinix.com, with replay options available afterward.

feed.title: Japan Post (NYSE: AFL) trims AFLAC stake with 14,700-share sale
feed.summary: Japan Post Holdings Co., Ltd., a major owner of AFLAC Inc., reported indirect open-market sales of 14,700 shares of Common Stock on March 30, 2026. The shares were sold in various transactions with weighted average prices ranging between $106.81-$107.64 and $107.86-$108.85 per share. Following these transactions, the reporting structure, through the J&A Alliance Trust, still holds 51,974,500 shares of AFLAC common stock indirectly.

feed.title: MEDIA ALERT: Equinix Sets Conference Call for First-Quarter Results
feed.summary: Equinix, Inc. (Nasdaq: EQIX) announced it will hold its quarterly conference call on Wednesday, April 29, 2026, at 5:30 p.m. ET to discuss its first-quarter results for the period ended March 31, 2026. Interested parties can access the call live via phone or webcast, with a replay available until June 30, 2026.

feed.title: NYSE Content Update: Versigent To Ring Opening Bell As its Shares Debut for Trade
feed.summary: Versigent (NYSE: VGNT) will ring the opening bell at the New York Stock Exchange as its shares begin trading following its spinoff from Aptiv (NYSE: APTV). CEO Joseph Liotine will join NYSE Live, along with TD Synnex (NYSE: SNX) CFO David Jordan discussing the company's financial results. The update also mentions other market activities including Blue Water Acquisition IV (NYSE: BWIV U) ringing the closing bell.

feed.title: Masimo (MASI) agrees to $180 cash takeover by Danaher; vote set May 1, 2026
feed.summary: Masimo Corporation has agreed to be acquired by Danaher Corporation for $180.00 per share in an all-cash merger, valuing Masimo at approximately $9.26 billion. The acquisition, which represents a 38% premium over Masimo's closing price on February 13, 2026, requires Masimo stockholder approval, with a special meeting scheduled for May 1, 2026. The Masimo Board unanimously recommends stockholders vote "FOR" the merger.

feed.title: Booking Holdings appoints ex-NXP Semiconductors CEO to board
feed.summary: Booking Holdings Inc. has appointed Kurt Sievers, former CEO of NXP Semiconductors, to its Board of Directors. Sievers brings decades of experience at the intersection of technology, mobility, and global consumer markets. This announcement comes as Director Lynn Radakovich prepares to retire, and Booking Holdings navigates a dynamic environment influenced by AI advancements and regulatory scrutiny, while its stock is considered undervalued by InvestingPro analysis.

feed.title: Franklin Templeton to acquire crypto firm 250 Digital
feed.summary: Franklin Templeton announced its plan to acquire 250 Digital, a cryptocurrency investment management firm, integrating its team and liquid crypto strategies. The acquisition, expected to close in Q2 2026, will establish a new Franklin Crypto unit led by Christopher Perkins and Seth Ginns. The deal includes BENJI tokens as payment, and Franklin Templeton will invest in the acquired strategies, further expanding its digital assets under management.

feed.title: Inka Health partners with OneMedNet for oncology data access
feed.summary: Inka Health, a subsidiary of Onco-Innovations Limited, has partnered with OneMedNet to access its iRWD platform, powered by Palantir Foundry, for U.S. real-world oncology data. This collaboration aims to support the development of Onco-Innovations' PNKP Inhibitor Technology for PTEN/SHP1-deficient cancers and to power Inka Health's AI platform, SynoGraph. Despite this strategic partnership, OneMedNet faces financial challenges, including being overvalued and quickly burning through cash, as highlighted by InvestingPro.

feed.title: Intel to buy back Apollo’s 49% stake in Irish fab for $14.2B
feed.summary: Intel Corporation announced it will repurchase Apollo’s 49% equity interest in the joint venture for its Fab 34 in Ireland for $14.2 billion. This move comes after Apollo-managed funds acquired the stake in 2024 for $11.2 billion, providing Intel with capital to advance its process technologies. Intel plans to finance the buyback using cash on hand and approximately $6.5 billion in new debt, expecting the transaction to boost its earnings per share and strengthen its credit profile.

feed.title: PYPL Investor Alert: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP Encourages PYPL Investors With Losses to Contact the Firm
feed.summary: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP has filed a securities fraud class action lawsuit against PayPal Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: PYPL) on behalf of investors who purchased common stock between February 25, 2025, and February 2, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that PayPal made misleading statements regarding its projected revenue and growth, failing to disclose material adverse facts. Investors who suffered losses are encouraged to contact the firm to discuss recovery options and to potentially file for lead plaintiff status by April 20, 2026.

feed.title: Tapcheck Teams with Paychex to Deliver Seamless Embedded On-Demand Pay Solution Giving Instant Access to Worksite Employees
feed.summary: Tapcheck has partnered with Paychex to offer an embedded on-demand pay solution, allowing employees paid through Paychex HR PEO to access their earned wages instantly. This integration aims to enhance financial wellness, reduce administrative burden for employers, and improve employee retention without requiring new systems or process changes. The solution provides greater flexibility and control over cash flow for worksite employees.

feed.title: CarMax made Fortune’s best workplaces list for the 22nd straight year
feed.summary: CarMax (NYSE: KMX) has been recognized on Fortune’s 100 Best Companies to Work For® list for the 22nd consecutive year, ranking #56 in 2026. The company highlights its competitive pay, benefits, and positive workplace culture, which is determined by anonymous employee surveys. CarMax is also actively hiring for various roles across its retail and corporate operations.

feed.title: Reagan Calhoun Joins Coke Florida as Vice President and Chief of Staff
feed.summary: Coca-Cola Beverages Florida (Coke Florida) has appointed Reagan Calhoun as Vice President and Chief of Staff to Chairman & CEO Troy Taylor. Calhoun brings over two decades of experience within The Coca-Cola Company system, holding previous roles such as Vice President, Finance for Marketing and Supply Chain within the North America Operating Unit. Her appointment is expected to strengthen leadership alignment and accelerate progress on key priorities for Coke Florida.

feed.title: Teledyne secures new Royal Navy contract for ocean data technology
feed.summary: Teledyne Marine has secured a contract with the UK Ministry of Defence to enhance the Royal Navy's ability to collect oceanographic and environmental data. This agreement, part of the Future Maritime Data Gathering (FMDG) programme, will supply autonomous ocean systems like Sentinel and Slocum gliders and APEX floats to support operational planning and defence activities. The initiative aims to improve understanding of the underwater battlespace in the North Atlantic and expands the Royal Navy's use of uncrewed technologies, continuing an existing relationship with Teledyne Marine dating back to 2015.

feed.title: Snap-on Werkzeugwagen: Professional Tool Storage Solutions Driving Efficiency in North American Automotive and Industrial Markets
feed.summary: Snap-on's Werkzeugwagen series provides essential and durable tool storage solutions for professional mechanics and industrial technicians, with demand driven by growth in automotive repair volumes, particularly for electric and hybrid vehicles. These premium tool carts feature robust construction, customizable drawers, and ergonomic designs, contributing significantly to efficiency in workshops and supporting Snap-on Inc.'s revenue and market position in North America. The product line is noted for its high-load capacity, lifetime warranty, and advanced features like integrated power strips and IoT capabilities, ensuring strong customer retention and stable contribution to Snap-on's financial performance.

feed.title: Surprising Beneficiaries of High Gas Prices: BJs and Costco
feed.summary: The article "Surprising Beneficiaries of High Gas Prices: BJs and Costco" explores how these warehouse retailers might unexpectedly profit from increasing fuel costs. It suggests a potential shift in consumer behavior driven by the need to save money wherever possible.

feed.title: Verizon Stock Is Up 25% in the Past 3 Months. Here’s What the Valuation Says Now
feed.summary: Verizon Communications (VZ) stock has surged by over 25% in the last three months due to improved subscriber trends, broadband additions, and new management optimism. While the company's turnaround story is gaining traction, TIKR's valuation model suggests a modest potential upside of 6.1% to $53 per share by December 2028, or a 2.2% annualized return. This indicates that much of the near-term improvement may already be priced into the stock, and future gains will likely depend more on execution than a re-rating.

feed.title: Ulta Names Kristin Wolf Chief Strategy & Growth Officer
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty has appointed Kristin Wolf as its new Chief Strategy & Growth Officer, effective March 2026. In this role, Wolf will oversee Ulta's corporate strategy, corporate development, and efforts to build businesses in new growth segments, including international expansion and the company’s enterprise AI strategy. Wolf brings over two decades of experience in the consumer and retail industries and has been with Ulta Beauty since 2019, where she played a key role in the "Ulta Beauty Unleashed" strategy and the launch of the wellness category.

feed.title: Stocks making big moves yesterday: Chewy, Broadcom, Microsoft, RXO, and JPMorgan Chase
feed.summary: This article highlights several companies whose stocks made significant moves yesterday, April 1, 2026. Chewy, Broadcom, Microsoft, RXO, and JPMorgan Chase all saw their stock prices rise due to specific company announcements or broader market conditions. Each company's rise is attributed to distinct positive news, ranging from geopolitical developments to strategic investments and new initiatives.

feed.title: Oncolytics completes reincorporation from Canada to Nevada By Investing.com
feed.summary: Oncolytics Biotech Inc. has completed its reincorporation from Canada to Nevada, a move approved by shareholders to reflect its status as a U.S. domestic issuer. The company, which develops an immunotherapeutic agent for cancer treatment, will maintain its Calgary office but establish San Diego as its new headquarters. This reincorporation is expected to streamline regulatory structure and improve access to U.S. capital markets, despite the company's recent stock decline and unprofitability predictions by analysts.

feed.title: Booking Holdings appoints ex-NXP Semiconductors CEO to board By Investing.com
feed.summary: Booking Holdings Inc. has appointed Kurt Sievers, the former CEO of NXP Semiconductors, to its Board of Directors. Sievers' extensive experience in technology, mobility, and consumer markets is expected to bring valuable perspective to the company. Concurrently, Director Lynn Radakovich will retire from the Board in June after a decade of service.

feed.title: BMO Capital Adjusts Constellation Energy Price Target to $386 From $410, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: BMO Capital has revised its price target for Constellation Energy (CEG) to $386, down from the previous $410, while reaffirming its "Outperform" rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other analyst revisions, including KeyBanc and Wells Fargo also lowering their price targets for the energy company. Despite these target reductions, the general analyst consensus remains a "BUY" for Constellation Energy.

feed.title: Oric Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:ORIC) Given New $17.00 Price Target at Wedbush
feed.summary: Wedbush has lowered its price target for Oric Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:ORIC) from $20.00 to $17.00 while maintaining an "outperform" rating, indicating a potential upside of approximately 34%. Despite this adjustment, the stock maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating among analysts with an average price target of $19.90. Insider transactions show the company's CFO sold a significant portion of shares, though institutional investors hold a substantial 95.05% of the stock.

feed.title: International Paper (NYSE:IP) Earns Hold Rating from Analysts at Deutsche Bank Aktiengesellschaft
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank Aktiengesellschaft initiated coverage on International Paper (NYSE:IP) with a "hold" rating and a $38 price target, indicating a potential upside of 6.06%. While the company missed its quarterly EPS estimates, its revenue increased by 53.1% year-over-year. Recent insider buying by the CEO and a director signals some confidence, despite the stock's negative net margin and PE ratio.

feed.title: Huntington Ingalls Industries (NYSE:HII) Coverage Initiated by Analysts at Wells Fargo & Company
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has initiated coverage on Huntington Ingalls Industries (NYSE:HII) with an "equal weight" rating and a $400.00 price target, suggesting a 5.49% upside. This comes after HII reported strong earnings, beating expectations with $4.04 EPS and $3.48 billion in revenue. The consensus among analysts is a "Moderate Buy," with an average target price of $385.89, although some insiders have recently sold shares.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company Initiates Coverage on General Dynamics (NYSE:GD)
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has initiated coverage on General Dynamics (NYSE:GD) with an "overweight" rating and a $400 price target, suggesting a 16.4% upside. This aligns with a broader analyst consensus of a "Moderate Buy" for the aerospace company. While the company recently beat EPS and revenue expectations and provided strong FY2026 guidance, insiders sold over 51,000 shares worth approximately $18.1 million in the last three months.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company Begins Coverage on Textron (NYSE:TXT)
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has initiated coverage on Textron (NYSE:TXT) with an "equal weight" rating and a $92 price target, suggesting a 5.06% upside. This comes as Textron reported Q4 revenues of $4.18 billion and EPS of $1.73, while also setting FY2026 guidance and seeing notable insider stock sales. Analyst consensus on Textron is currently a "Hold" with an average target of $98.

feed.title: T. Rowe Price Group (NASDAQ:TROW) Price Target Lowered to $107.00 at Morgan Stanley
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has lowered its price target for T. Rowe Price Group (NASDAQ:TROW) to $107.00 from $115.00, maintaining an "equal weight" rating. This adjustment follows T. Rowe Price missing its Q4 EPS estimates, reporting $2.44 against an estimated $2.47, though revenue slightly exceeded expectations at $1.93 billion. Several other firms have also trimmed their price targets, contributing to a consensus "Reduce" rating and an average target price of $101.08 for the asset manager.

feed.title: Transdigm Group (NYSE:TDG) Coverage Initiated at Wells Fargo & Company
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company initiated coverage on Transdigm Group (NYSE:TDG) with an "equal weight" rating and a $1,200 price target, suggesting a 3.45% upside. The aerospace company recently beat its quarterly earnings expectations and provided strong FY2026 guidance. While institutional ownership is high and increasing, there has been significant insider selling of the stock.

feed.title: 12 Michigan counties move to one emergency dispatch platform
feed.summary: Tyler Technologies has secured a deal with Michigan’s Upper Peninsula 911 Authority, representing 12 counties, to implement its Enterprise CAD and Enterprise Mobile solutions powered by AWS. This initiative aims to unify emergency communications across these counties, enhancing coordination, situational awareness, and interoperability for public safety operations. The cloud-based platform, hosted in AWS GovCloud (US), will streamline cross-jurisdictional communication and provide first responders with faster access to critical information.
